title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 June 2026.

items.title: Mario Rabbids creator’s (Davide Soliani) **Bradley the Badger** from studio Day 4 Night signed by Focus Entertainment
**Xbox** is exploring ‘radically different business models’ to make its next console (Project Helix) more affordable  amid ongoing hardware crisis
Physical **Steam** gift cards are being ended by Valve because scammers keep using them to trick people

The UK-based semiconductor and software design company **Arm** has revealed the game Neural Dawn (developed with UK studio Sumo Digital), which takes advantage of Arm's neural graphics technology to allow the use of Unreal Engine MegaLights on mobile devices by using the Arm Mali GPU's neural technology
bullets: The intent for the technology is for Arm is to offer "desktop-class" visuals whilst maintaining battery life

items.title: UK co-development studio **CodeDev** Launches ‘The Performance Guys’ and Announces Hunt for Senior Optimisation Talent
**Ubisoft** announces approximately 380 employees have been impacted by restructuring changes
bullets: Insider Gaming claims the affected studios / projects are:

bullets.text: Ubisoft Winnipeg, 65 employees laid off, and the studio closed
Ubisoft Belgrade, approximately 100 employees were laid off, and the studio closed
Rainbow Six Siege, 120 employees ramped off
Ubisoft San Francisco, undisclosed number of employees laid off

bullets: Insider Gaming also claims that Ubisoft initially embargoed news to outlets about the layoffs and studio closures

items.title: **Frontier Developments** reported its second-highest revenue to date, rising 16% to £104.8 million in FY26
bullets: Operating profit increased by 44% to £19 million, surpassing the previous record of £15.9 million set in FY19
The UK-based studio credited its 'strategic focus' on creative management simulation (CMS) games for this growth, with the genre now accounting for 90% of FY26 revenue

items.title: **Xbox** is planning major layoffs next month as new CEO Asha Sharma confronts a bleak picture and plans what she calls a "reset" of the business
bullets: In an email sent to employees on Wednesday, CEO Asha Sharma said that the business had plummeted to a 3% “accountability margin,” the metric Microsoft uses to reflect profit margin.

bullets.text: Also in that email, Asha Sharma said “Excluding Activision Blizzard King, over the past five years, we have spent over $20 billion on ongoing investments in our content, platform and hardware subsidy, but our annual revenue has declined nearly half a billion during that time. Going forward, this cannot continue.”
